records.summary: A post-procedure coronary angiogram still image showing the left circumflex artery (LCX) with stent placement, annotated as 'LCX POST STENT FLOW WITH 1DES'. The view is from a caudal 38° angle, acquired on 03/31/2026.
key_observations: Image is a fluoroscopy frame from a cath-lab monitor.
Annotation confirms post-stent flow in the LCX artery.

records.stage_evidence: Report describes 'Successful Adhoc PTCA to LCX done with 1DES' and details stent deployment with TIMI III flow achieved.

ocr.verbatim:
DEPARTMENT OF CARDIOLOGY
IPD. No. : 07362
Patient Name : 
Procedure Date : 31/03/2026
Consultant/Operator : 
CORONARY ANGIOPLASTY REPORT
Hemodynamic Status :
Pressure : 140/70mm of Hg
HR : 64 bpm
SPo2 : 99%
Approach : Right Radial Approach
Diagnosis : CAD-ACS-IWMI, MODERATE LV DYSFUNCTION
CAG-SVD
Lesion : LCX mid 90% stenosis.
Hardware Used:
Guiding Catheter: 6F EBU 3.5
PTCA wire: Fielder FC
Balloon : Zee Pro SC 2.50x10mm & Zee Pro NC 3.0x10mm
stent: Racer CC Plus 3.0x21mm
Procedure: Adhoc PTCA to LCX
Left main Coronary artery engaged with 6F- EBU 3.5 guiding catheter. Lesion crossed with Fielder FC
Wire. Lesion was pre dilated with Zee Pro SC 2.50x10mm balloon at 12atms pressure. Lesion was stented
with Racer CC Plus 3.0x21mm stent at 10atms pressure. Stent was Post dilated with Zee Pro NC
3.0x10mm Balloons at 14 atms pressure. Good end result with TIMI III flow achieved.
CONCLUSION : Successful Adhoc PTCA to LCX done with 1DES.
Important note::
1. Never stop medicines without written advice of doctor,
2. Consult doctor if chest pain/ Breathing difficulty/any other symptoms,
3. Do regular follow up.
4. Stents carry an inherent 5 % risk of restenosis.
MD(PUL.MED). DrNB(Cardiology)
Consultant Interventional Cardiologist

records.summary: This is a typed Coronary Angioplasty Report detailing a successful percutaneous coronary intervention (PTCA) on the left circumflex artery (LCX) using a drug-eluting stent (DES). The procedure was performed via a right radial approach on March 31, 2026. The report includes hemodynamic status, lesion details, hardware used, procedural steps, and post-procedure outcome (TIMI III flow).
key_observations: Procedure: Adhoc PTCA to LCX with stent placement (Racer CC Plus 3.0x21mm)
Successful outcome: TIMI III flow achieved
Stent risk: 5% inherent risk of restenosis

records.stage_evidence: Conclusion states 'Successful Adhoc PTCA to LCX done with 1DES' and shows a stent deployed in LCX

records.summary: This is an operation note for a percutaneous transluminal coronary angioplasty (PTCA) procedure performed on a 47-year-old male patient. The procedure targeted a 90% stenosis in the mid-left circumflex (LCX) coronary artery. A Fielder FC wire, Zee ProSC balloon for pre-dilation, a Racer CC Plus stent, and a Zee Pro NC balloon for post-dilation were used. The procedure was successful, achieving TIMI III flow, and the patient is advised medical management.
key_observations: Hardware used: Fielder FC wire, Zee ProSC 2.50x10mm balloon, Racer CC Plus 3.0x21mm stent, Zee Pro NC 3.0x10mm balloon
Outcome: Successful PTCA with TIMI III flow achieved

records.stage_evidence: Annotation overlay 'LCX MID 90% STENOSIS' and procedure code 'CAG+PTCA/1915+1916' indicate active intervention

records.summary: This is a typed coronary angiography report from the Department of Cardiology. It details hemodynamic status, vessel findings (LMCA, LAD, LCX, RCA), and concludes with a diagnosis of CAD-ACS-IWMI and moderate LV dysfunction, advising an ad hoc PTCA to the LCX with 1DES.
key_observations: 90% stenosis in the dominant LCX artery (lesion length 3.0x21mm)
Recommendation: Ad hoc PTCA to LCX with 1DES

records.stage_evidence: Handwritten annotation 'stent' near LCX, 'SVD' in conclusion, 'P.TCA to LCx' in advice

records.summary: This is an operation note from a coronary angiography procedure performed on March 31, 2026. It documents the coronary anatomy, findings of significant stenosis in the left circumflex artery (LCX), and concludes with a diagnosis of CAD-ACS-IUMI with moderate left ventricular dysfunction. The advice is to perform PTCA to the LCX with a 1 DES.
key_observations: Advice: Athoc PTCA to LCX with 1 DES
